pi_items.source: Python analysis showing actual 16-bit WM mean is ~600, which satisfies the >100 minimum.
Python analysis showing t1-head.tif is 16-bit with WM mean ~600, contradicting the 250 maximum claim.
Python analysis showing actual 16-bit GM mean is ~270, which satisfies the >50 minimum.
Python analysis showing t1-head.tif is 16-bit with GM mean ~270, contradicting the 200 maximum claim.
Dataset documentation and Python analysis showing voxels outside the head are exactly 0.0.
Dataset documentation and Python analysis showing voxels outside the head are exactly 0.0.
Division by zero. Std_BG is 0.0, causing mathematical Infinity.
Division by zero. Std_BG is 0.0, causing mathematical Infinity.

privileged_info_summary: The target dataset 'T1 Head (2.4M)' (t1-head.zip) is a real 16-bit MRI volume from the University of Waterloo where the background voxels outside the head have been artificially set to exactly zero (as documented in t1-head.txt) to improve compression. As a result, the measured background standard deviation (`Std_BG`) will be exactly 0.0. This exposes a critical bug in the task: the verifier deducts 10 points for `std_bg > 0` failing, and any resulting 'Infinity' or 'NaN' outputs for SNR and CNR are ignored by the export script's regex (`r"[-+]?\d*\.\d+|\d+"`), resulting in an automatic loss of another 40 points. Additionally, because the image is 16-bit, the actual mean intensities for White Matter (~600) and Gray Matter (~270) significantly exceed the metadata claims (`wm_mean_max`: 250, `gm_mean_max`: 200) which falsely assume an 8-bit scale.
